claims.claim: Unilateral child conversion ban enforcement lacking in 6 states
claims.source: The Sun/Bernama, 3 Feb 2026, reporting High Court proceedings: https://thesun.my/news/malaysia-news/courts/high-court-to-rule-on-indira-gandhis-unilateral-conversion-challenge-in-may/ ; Indira Gandhi Federal Court judgment [2018] 1 MLJ 545: https://www.defendingforb.org/media/flzf1euq/indira-gandhi-ap-mutho-v-pengarah-jabatan-agama-islam-perak-ors-and-other-appeals-2018-1-mlj-545.pdf
claims.correction: The available reporting supports an ongoing legal challenge against laws in six states and the Federal Territories, not simply six states. 'Enforcement lacking' is a broad characterisation; the more precise claim is that certain state/Federal Territories provisions were alleged in court to permit unilateral conversion despite the Indira Gandhi ruling.
claims.stale_check: Not stale in the direction claimed: a 3 Feb 2026 Bernama report still described a challenge against six states and the Federal Territories.

claims.claim: Despite the 2018 Federal Court ruling requiring both parents' consent for child conversion, 6 states had not amended their enactments by 2025.
claims.source: Indira Gandhi Federal Court judgment [2018] 1 MLJ 545, paras 181 and 185: https://www.defendingforb.org/media/flzf1euq/indira-gandhi-ap-mutho-v-pengarah-jabatan-agama-islam-perak-ors-and-other-appeals-2018-1-mlj-545.pdf ; The Sun/Bernama, 3 Feb 2026: https://thesun.my/news/malaysia-news/courts/high-court-to-rule-on-indira-gandhis-unilateral-conversion-challenge-in-may/
claims.correction: The first half is verified. The exact '6 states had not amended by 2025' claim could not be verified from primary state gazettes or official legal texts within this check. Public court reporting in 2026 says a suit targets six states and the Federal Territories.
claims.stale_check: A 2026 report suggests the issue remained live after 2025, but the exact 2025 amendment status requires official state-law verification.

claims.claim: 6 states have not amended laws to comply with 2018 Indira Gandhi ruling
claims.correction: Use 'six states and the Federal Territories were defendants in a 2023-originating legal challenge over laws alleged to permit unilateral conversion' unless official gazettes for each state are checked.
claims.stale_check: The 2026 court report indicates no obvious stale correction that would make the number lower by then, but it also includes the Federal Territories.

claims.claim: Unilateral child conversion still possible in states that ignore Federal Court
claims.source: Federal Court in Loh Siew Hong matter, reported by The Edge Malaysia, 14 May 2024: https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/711483 ; Indira Gandhi Federal Court judgment [2018] 1 MLJ 545: https://www.defendingforb.org/media/flzf1euq/indira-gandhi-ap-mutho-v-pengarah-jabatan-agama-islam-perak-ors-and-other-appeals-2018-1-mlj-545.pdf
claims.correction: The phrase 'ignore Federal Court' overstates intent. The verified position is that the Indira Gandhi ruling is binding nationwide and later courts applied it, including against Perlis section 117(b). Whether administrative conversion is 'still possible' depends on current state procedures and litigation outcomes.
claims.stale_check: The May 2024 Federal Court refusal of leave in Loh reinforced that Indira Gandhi is binding nationwide, weakening any claim that state law can lawfully override it.

claims.claim: Federal Court in Indira Gandhi 2018 ruled both parents must consent to conversion
claims.source: Indira Gandhi a/p Mutho v Pengarah Jabatan Agama Islam Perak & Ors and other appeals [2018] 1 MLJ 545, Federal Court judgment, paras 181 and 185: https://www.defendingforb.org/media/flzf1euq/indira-gandhi-ap-mutho-v-pengarah-jabatan-agama-islam-perak-ors-and-other-appeals-2018-1-mlj-545.pdf
claims.correction: More precise wording: the Federal Court held both parents' consent was required before certificates of conversion to Islam could be issued for children of the civil marriage in that case, interpreting Article 12(4) with the Eleventh Schedule and the Guardianship of Infants Act.
claims.stale_check: Not stale; the Federal Court in May 2024 refused leave in Loh Siew Hong and treated Indira Gandhi as binding nationwide.

claims.claim: State syariah enactments in 6 states still permit single-parent conversion
claims.source: The Sun/Bernama, 3 Feb 2026: https://thesun.my/news/malaysia-news/courts/high-court-to-rule-on-indira-gandhis-unilateral-conversion-challenge-in-may/ ; Malay Mail/Yahoo, 20 Mar 2023, listing challenged provisions: https://malaysia.news.yahoo.com/conversion-islam-without-both-parents-050543166.html
claims.correction: Could not independently verify all six current state enactments from official gazettes. The safer wording is that plaintiffs challenged provisions in six states and the Federal Territories that they allege are inconsistent with Article 12(4) as interpreted in Indira Gandhi.
claims.stale_check: Pahang appears to have been removed from the suit after amending its law, according to contemporaneous court reporting, so older 'eight laws' counts may be stale.

claims.claim: Article 12(4) places a child's religion in the parent or guardian's hands until 18
claims.source: Federal Constitution text, Article 12(4), Judicial Appointments Commission PDF: https://jac.gov.my/sites/default/files/documents/Federal%20Constitution.pdf ; Indira Gandhi judgment quoting Article 12(4), para 148: https://www.defendingforb.org/media/flzf1euq/indira-gandhi-ap-mutho-v-pengarah-jabatan-agama-islam-perak-ors-and-other-appeals-2018-1-mlj-545.pdf
claims.correction: The text says the religion of a person under 18 shall be decided by his parent or guardian for the purposes of Article 12(3). The Indira Gandhi ruling interprets 'parent' as both parents where both are alive.

claims.claim: Indira Gandhi 2018 read 'parent' as both parents; 6 state enactments still allow one
claims.source: Indira Gandhi judgment, paras 172 and 181: https://www.defendingforb.org/media/flzf1euq/indira-gandhi-ap-mutho-v-pengarah-jabatan-agama-islam-perak-ors-and-other-appeals-2018-1-mlj-545.pdf ; The Sun/Bernama, 3 Feb 2026: https://thesun.my/news/malaysia-news/courts/high-court-to-rule-on-indira-gandhis-unilateral-conversion-challenge-in-may/
claims.correction: The Indira Gandhi interpretation is verified. The '6 state enactments still allow one' portion was not fully verified from primary state law texts and should be qualified.
claims.stale_check: No contradiction found, but the ongoing 2026 litigation means the legal status was still contested.

claims.claim: 23 new unilateral conversion disputes filed in courts between 2019 and 2024
claims.source: No primary court filing database, judgment list, or official statistic verifying '23 new disputes' was found. DFAT Country Information Report Malaysia, 24 Jun 2024, only says cases persist and cites the Loh Siew Hong matter: https://www.dfat.gov.au/sites/default/files/malaysia-dfat-country-information-report-24-june-2024.pdf
claims.correction: Remove the number unless a case list or official court registry source is provided. A defensible replacement is: 'Post-Indira unilateral-conversion litigation continued, including the Loh Siew Hong case and the 2023 challenge by Indira Gandhi and 13 others.'
claims.stale_check: No stale issue; the claim is simply unverified.

claims.claim: Children remain caught between state non-compliance and federal ruling
claims.source: DFAT Country Information Report Malaysia, 24 Jun 2024: https://www.dfat.gov.au/sites/default/files/malaysia-dfat-country-information-report-24-june-2024.pdf ; Federal Court Loh Siew Hong leave decision as reported by The Edge Malaysia, 14 May 2024: https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/711483
claims.correction: There is evidence that disputes persist, but 'state non-compliance' is a legal conclusion that should be tied to specific provisions and court findings. For Perlis, section 117(b) was ruled unconstitutional and that outcome stood after the Federal Court refused leave.
claims.stale_check: The May 2024 Loh Federal Court development should be included because it materially clarifies the post-2018 legal position.

claims.claim: When states ignore the Federal Court for 7 years, constitutional supremacy erodes
claims.source: Indira Gandhi judgment, conclusion and prospective effect, paras 185-187: https://www.defendingforb.org/media/flzf1euq/indira-gandhi-ap-mutho-v-pengarah-jabatan-agama-islam-perak-ors-and-other-appeals-2018-1-mlj-545.pdf ; The Edge Malaysia, 14 May 2024: https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/711483
claims.correction: This is opinion framed as fact. It should be rewritten as an analytical view: 'Continued provisions alleged to conflict with Indira Gandhi raise constitutional-supremacy concerns.'
claims.stale_check: Not stale, but it omits the 2024 Federal Court reinforcement in Loh Siew Hong.

claims.claim: Attorney General issued reminder to all states to comply in November 2024
claims.source: No primary AGC statement, parliamentary answer, gazette, or official release from November 2024 was found. Related 2024/2026 reporting mentions AGC/court positions but not this claimed reminder.
claims.correction: Remove unless the AGC statement or parliamentary record is supplied. If referring to a court submission, identify the case, date, and exact statement.
claims.stale_check: No evidence found that a later official reminder made this claim true.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: 23 new unilateral conversion disputes filed in courts between 2019 and 2024
cardinal_sin_flags.why: A precise number and date range are asserted, but no official court registry, judgment list, or primary statistical source was found.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: No primary source found; DFAT 24 Jun 2024 only states cases persist: https://www.dfat.gov.au/sites/default/files/malaysia-dfat-country-information-report-24-june-2024.pdf

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Attorney General issued reminder to all states to comply in November 2024
cardinal_sin_flags.why: No AGC release, parliamentary record, gazette, or other primary source verifying this reminder was found.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Unilateral child conversion still possible in states that ignore Federal Court
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The phrase imputes intentional disregard and may imply lawful effectiveness despite the Federal Court's binding nationwide position and later Loh Siew Hong decisions.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: The Edge Malaysia, 14 May 2024, reporting Federal Court refusal of leave and nationwide binding effect: https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/711483

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: 6 states have not amended laws to comply with 2018 Indira Gandhi ruling
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The available court reporting identifies six states and the Federal Territories in litigation; the analysis states six states only and does not prove current amendment status from official state-law sources.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: When states ignore the Federal Court for 7 years, constitutional supremacy erodes
cardinal_sin_flags.why: This is a normative conclusion presented as factual causation. It omits the 2024 Federal Court reinforcement of Indira Gandhi in Loh Siew Hong.

omitted_facts: The Federal Court stated the Indira Gandhi ruling has prospective effect, so it was not intended to retrospectively undo prior court decisions: Indira Gandhi judgment paras 187-188, https://www.defendingforb.org/media/flzf1euq/indira-gandhi-ap-mutho-v-pengarah-jabatan-agama-islam-perak-ors-and-other-appeals-2018-1-mlj-545.pdf

In May 2024, the Federal Court refused MAIPs' leave application in the Loh Siew Hong case, leaving standing the Court of Appeal ruling that Perlis section 117(b) was unconstitutional and invalid: https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/711483

The later court challenge reported in February 2026 is against six states and the Federal Territories, not just six states: https://thesun.my/news/malaysia-news/courts/high-court-to-rule-on-indira-gandhis-unilateral-conversion-challenge-in-may/

Pahang was reportedly removed from earlier litigation after amending its state law to prevent unilateral conversion; this affects older counts of affected jurisdictions and should be checked against official Pahang gazettes before publication.
source_assessment: The original analysis cites only 'T4A related issue 1960', which is circular and not a primary source. It does not cite the Indira Gandhi judgment, the Federal Constitution, state enactments, gazettes, AGC statements, court filings, or later Loh Siew Hong rulings. The source range is therefore extremely narrow, with no visible government, court, state, affected-community, Islamic council, legal expert, or opposing-party primary material.
overall_assessment: The core legal proposition that Indira Gandhi requires both parents' consent is well supported by the Federal Court judgment and later reinforced in Loh Siew Hong. However, several publication-critical details are not adequately verified: the exact count of six states, the omission of the Federal Territories, the '23 disputes' statistic, and the claimed November 2024 Attorney General reminder. The analysis should be treated as legally plausible but factually under-sourced and partly over-framed until those specifics are replaced with primary-source-backed wording.
